title: pediomelum hypogaeum var. subulatum
intro: Pediomelum hypogaeum var. subulatum, also known as a variant of the sand-verbena or prairie turnip, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. The plant is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues and respiratory problems. The health benefits of Pediomelum hypogaeum var. subulatum include its anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, which can aid in reducing swelling and preventing infection. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as teas and infusions, can be made by drying the roots and leaves to use as a natural remedy for various ailments.
